title: Using 'Saber' and Related Structures
description: Learn to use 'saber' (to know) and related phrases in Spanish.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: To know in Spanish is saber.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Saber. How would you say 'I want to know'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'I don't want to know'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'he wants to know' or 'she wants to know'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Why doesn't he want to know?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: I want to know why he doesn't want to know:

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: quiero saber por qué no quiere saber

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Very good. The word for 'yes' in Spanish is 'sí'. This is also the word for 'if'. How do you say 'if'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'I want to know if you want to know' (speaking formally):

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'I want to know if he wants to invite me'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'I don't want to imagine it':

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'I want to know if she wants to come to the house to visit me':

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: quiero saber si quiere venir a la casa a visitarme

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Very good. Let's practice a listening exercise. What does 'Voy a invitarlo, pero no voy a invitarlo ahora' mean?

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: I'm going to invite him, but I won't invite him now.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How do you say 'justification' in Spanish?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'I'm not trying to justify myself, but I want to explain something to you' (informally):

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: no intento justificarme, pero quiero explicarte algo

outro.text: You've learned how to use 'saber' and related phrases to express knowledge and curiosity in Spanish.
Try forming your own sentences using 'saber' and other verbs you've learned. Practice makes perfect!
Remember, breaking down sentences into smaller parts can help you construct more complex ideas in Spanish.
